commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Queue a narrow repair because the blocker is a concrete predicate-and-test gap rather than a broad product decision.; Needs attention: The diff narrows a security-relevant startup warning and misses a documented Host-header fallback exposure signal.; Review finding: [P2] Keep the warning for host-header fallback — src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts:249-253 (sha=9a0b8876803a1cbdd0a6073ea2c0c0393db4bf19)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway startup logging so loopback-only no-auth gateway runs do not emit the unauthenticated warning while exposed no-auth paths still do, with CLI tests for the new warning behavior.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +78. Total +104 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main warns unconditionally for resolvedAuthMode none, and the PR-introduced missed case follows from loopback auth.mode none plus gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback=true. I did not execute the CLI in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue a narrow repair because the blocker is a concrete predicate-and-test gap rather than a broad product decision.
